drink.name: Pistachio Latte
drink.reason: Specifically called out as 'incredibly tasty and creative' alongside the French Toast Cappuccino in a 5-star review. Other coffee mentions are generic, but this named specialty drink got direct praise.
drink.quote: incredibly tasty and creative

food.name: Israeli Breakfast
food.reason: Named and praised in two separate 5-star reviews as light, refreshing, and a perfect pick-me-up — the most consistently recommended dish.
food.quote: light, refreshing, and such a great pick-me-up

reviewSample.text:
One of the first places where I am genuinely impressed by the food in America. The French toast is light and refreshing and doesn’t feel thick. Same goes for the Acai.

My favourite was the Franks famous hash as it’s usually something I’m scared to order in a restaurant as it’s rarely nice. Delicious. Yum yum. Definitely recommending making the eggs poached and breaking them all over the meat so it’s juicy and has the extra flavour. Don’t even need the side sauce.

Coffee was also good, forgot to take the photo. Israeli breakfast also good. Seating areas inside and outside are both beautiful and the lady Madey who served us was really nice.

Overall would come back again and highly recommended! Also side note compulsory 20% trip to eat here.

We went there for brunch with my niece and had such a lovely experience. The food was absolutely delicious — everything was fresh, flavorful, and beautifully presented.

The service was excellent, and Juli, who took care of us, was absolutely lovely. She was kind, attentive, and made us feel very welcome. It’s a great spot for a special brunch that still feels comfortable and family-friendly. We’ll definitely be back!

Eeeee!! What a bangin' brunch spot!!! It was a nice, late morning, partly sunny, cool breeze - perfect for enjoying some delicious food and drinks outside. We started with a French Toast Cappuccino and Pistachio Latte, which were incredibly tasty and creative! Then we had the Steak & Eggs and Smoked Salmon Benedict with the most delicious crispy breakfast potatoes on the side. Our waitress, Maday, was super sweet and helpful, too. Overall a great dining experience I'd love to have again and again. This place will definitely be on the map for me if I'm ever in South Beach!

drink.name: Printed specialty coffee (cappuccino)
drink.reason: The printed-picture coffee is the cafe's signature, mentioned in nearly every review as memorable and tasty, with the vanilla version specifically praised.
drink.quote: do get your printed coffee! Not only it's aesthetic but also very tasty

food.name: Steak sandwich
food.reason: Specifically named and praised in two separate 5-star reviews for tender, flavorful steak and fresh bread.
food.quote: The steak was tender and flavorful, the bread was fresh

reviewSample.text: Omgggggg, my fave place so far in Miami for a yummy bite! Calista, my server was amazing by far, very helpful and attentive despite the busy brunch rush. The Mocha Frappe was good without being overly sweet. Orange Iced Tea was quite refreshing. The steak sandwich on croissant was delightful. Pancakes were fluffy and filling. The picture printing on the coffee of your choice was a fabulous, memorable touch. 1000/10, I would definitely recommend this cafe.

This was a great experience  me and my dad did a day trip to Miami where we celebrated his 60th birthday and we stopped by here. The staff was very friendly, very welcoming and offered us the best Customer Service that I’ve experienced in a while we were able to get our brunch.
Our portion size was big enough to share. We got our cappuccinos with our pictures printed on them and also charged our phones which was a plus since we were only in town for a day. I would recommend this restaurant to anyone that’s visiting the Miami area. Also, it’s attached to a beautiful, beautiful hotel in case you will be staying there. The bus comes in front of it and it’s also located very close to Collins Avenue and the beach. It’s a must try. I will give it a 10out of 10.

The restaurant/Cafe is around the corner from the Marriott Cadillac.
It has a small indoor dining room and a large patio with umbrellas.
The menu is relatively e tensive for a cafe.
Service is exceptional and very accommodating.
Their specialty coffees are tasty.
The various mains that we had were very fresh and tasty and special requests for modifications were accepted with a smile.
The freshly made beignets were hot and delicious.
Very relaxed lunch in a laid back environment.

drink.name: Dior Rose
drink.reason: Only specifically named drink, mentioned with enthusiasm in a 5-star review.
drink.quote: the Dior rose! Yummmm

food.name: Desserts
food.reason: Repeatedly praised across multiple 5-star reviews as delicious and beautifully presented, though no specific dessert is named.
food.quote: every bite felt like a little piece of art

reviewSample.text: Amazing greeting and experience! Energy was great! Service was top teir and the wait wasn’t bad at all about 20 mins. Simassimo (Not sure I spelled it right ) was absolutely my fav part from greeting to exit. The staff was attentive! Will be back oh and the Dior rose! Yummmm

We had such a wonderful experience at Café Dior!
The team is amazing — professional, friendly, and truly attentive. The service was absolutely impeccable.
The desserts were delicious and beautifully presented — every bite felt like a little piece of art.
And Massimo is simply outstanding! His energy, professionalism, and charm made our visit even more special.

Highly recommend — we will definitely be back! 💫

My first visit to the Dior Café in Miami was truly magical. ✨

From the moment you step inside, everything feels like a dream. The space is incredibly beautiful, elegant, and filled with that unmistakable Dior atmosphere where every detail is thoughtfully designed. It feels less like a café and more like stepping into a piece of art.

The desserts are not only delicious but also absolutely stunning. Each one looks like a tiny masterpiece. Every bite felt special, delicate, and perfectly balanced.

What made the experience even more wonderful was the service. The staff here is incredibly attentive and caring. I was lucky to be served by Julia, who was simply amazing. She was warm, professional, and made the whole visit feel even more special.

This was my very first time at a Dior Café, and it truly exceeded all expectations. An unforgettable experience in every way.

I will definitely be coming back. 🤍

drink.reason: Only negative drink mentions (Strawberry Matcha, Blue Pina Colada Smoothie called bland/mess) and one vague 'great coffee' mention. Not enough positive specific data.

food.name: MIAM Club Sandwich
food.reason: Singled out as the best item in an otherwise negative review; other positive reviews praise food generally but don't name specific dishes consistently. Steak and Eggs is a close alternative but only mentioned once.
food.quote: The MIAM Club Sandwich was the best out of all of the items we ordered

reviewSample.text:
My first impressions were confused. Immediately upon walking inside I’m unsure if I’m supposed grab a tale, wait to be sat down or order at the front. When I asked someone politely I was shrugged off. Then I figured it out after standing for 5 minutes like an idiot, the people taking orders had an attitude and was rude to people asking for their drinks. Then after ordering, you get a table number and then grab where ever you want to sit. The people bringing the food are “robotic” they don’t say anything and just set food down on the table

The food is mostly misses. The pancakes look appealing but were dry, potatoes had no flavor, and the eggs were eggs. The MIAM Club Sandwich was the best out of all of the items we ordered, and carry’s the whole rating for the Café.

The drinks we ordered were all misses. The Strawberry Matcha was very bland and the matcha is very thick. The Blue Pina Colada Smoothie doesn’t really taste like pina colada, tastes more like a mess.

I simply fell for the hype and left disappointed feeling I should have spent my $80 for 2 people somewhere else.

A Wynwood Staple: The Perfect Modern Brunch Spot
If you find yourself wandering the street art-filled blocks of Wynwood, there is one culinary staple that simply cannot be missed: Miam Cafe. Every single time my wife and I are in Miami, this is our mandatory stop. It has become a tradition for us, and for good reason—the consistency and quality never waver.
The aesthetic is very nice and modern, perfectly capturing that industrial-chic Wynwood energy with its clean lines and minimalist black-and-white decor. It’s the kind of bright, airy space that makes a morning meal feel special without being overly fussy. It’s located in the iconic Wynwood Garage building, which adds to the overall architectural appeal.
On our most recent visit, I opted for the Steak and Eggs, and it was exceptional. The steak was seasoned perfectly and cooked exactly to my liking, providing a hearty start to the day. My wife tried the Scrambled Eggs and Potato, which she absolutely loved. The eggs were light, fluffy, and seasoned just right, while the potatoes had that perfect golden crispness.
The food was great across the board, proving that Miam Cafe isn’t just about the "cool" atmosphere—they actually deliver on the plate. If you want a reliable, stylish, and delicious breakfast in one of Miami’s most vibrant neighborhoods, this is the place. We’ll definitely be back on our next trip!
Best breakfast place ever!!! Worth the money! U need to try it! Great service, great food, great coffee yum yum Miami! : ) ernesto our server was super attentive.

drink.name: Espresso
drink.reason: Mentioned as a good pairing with pastries; coffee otherwise gets only generic mentions.
drink.quote: cute place to grab a pastry and espresso

food.name: Pistachio croissant
food.reason: The only food item to receive specific, unambiguous praise across these mixed reviews, while pancakes were heavily criticized.
food.quote: the pistachio croissant was delicious and noticeably of high quality

reviewSample.text: Today is the second time y wife and I come to Miam Coral Gables. This time around though it was special as we were celebrating our daughter's birthday. We truly love everything Miam offers. The food is delicious. The coffee keeps you warm. And of course, we can't forget about our server, Keily. She is amazing and has a wonderful personality. Can't wait to come back again.

I had an outstanding experience at Miam Restaurant! From the moment I walked in, the atmosphere felt warm, inviting, and full of great energy. The food was absolutely delicious — every dish was fresh, flavorful, and beautifully presented.

But the highlight of my visit was our server, Daily. She was exceptional — friendly, attentive, and genuinely thoughtful. Daily checked on us at the perfect moments, made great recommendations, and created a welcoming vibe that took the whole experience to the next level. It’s rare to find service that feels this personal and consistent.

I’ll definitely be coming back, and I highly recommend Miam to anyone looking for great food, great service, and an all-around amazing dining experience. Thank you, Daily, for making it unforgettable!

Sooo… I’m gonna be honest here because there are A LOT of places to grab breakfast in the area and reviews are a great way to make an informed choice on where to spend your money. I just spent $75 ON BREAKFAST FOR ONE PERSON to give you this insight 😂. I’ve been wanting to try this place for ages since it always looks busy and aesthetically, those pancakes look bomb. I tried a little bit of everything including a specialty coffee drink, the famous pancakes, a croissant, and the bacon/eggs/home fries platter. While the pistachio croissant was delicious and noticeably of high quality, the rest of the food just isn’t worth the price tag. The food looked great but wasn’t very warm upon arrival to my table and lacked flavor or anything that would justify the insane price I paid for breakfast 🤪

Definitely a cute place to grab a pastry and espresso but not for a meal.  I do love that you order at the counter and they bring you your food and leave you be so you can have an uninterrupted conversations with whoever you’re with. Points for that!

drink.name: Cappuccino
drink.reason: Specifically praised as rich, smooth, and beautifully made in a 5-star review.
drink.quote: the cappuccino… rich, smooth, and beautifully made

food.name: Neapolitana pizza
food.reason: Pizza is praised across multiple reviews for its wood-fired flavor, and the Neapolitana with beet aioli was called 'INCREDIBLE.'
food.quote: The Neapolitana pizza with the beet aioli was INCREDIBLE

reviewSample.text: Looking for a casual lunch, we stumbled across this restaurant at the site of an old favorite of ours. The Provoleta arrived sizzling in a cast iron pan with crunchy, light bread. Incredible. The Caprese omelette came with ample Swiss cheese and a side salad. My Crispy Chicken Sandwich with extra crispy fries was unbelievable, one of the best I’ve had. Definitely give this place a try, you won’t regret it.

I had 75 guests from USA, Europe and LatAm for a wedding brunch this morning at Craft.  From the moment I booked to overstay 90 mins because of the rain; the staff was professional, courteous and welcoming.  Kudos to Facundo and PABLO!
I gave him a BIG HUG when I told him I was grateful he accommodated due to rain, he responded he was grateful to us.
I’m GRATEFUL for the Beautiful experience,  genuine hospitality and I forgot to mention the most important part, the food is ALWAYS AMAZING.
Craft you do things right!!
THANK YOU 🙏😘🥰❤️

Craft Miami exceeded my expectations! Our server Julieta was friendly and provided good recommendations.

The food was excellent. The pizza was perfectly crisp with that wood-fired flavor you can’t beat, and the Post-Apocalyptic Brunch plate was such a fun and flavorful combo, every bite hit! I also have to shout out the cappuccino… rich, smooth, and beautifully made.

drink.name: Cuban coffee
drink.reason: Multiple reviewers specifically came for and praised the Cuban coffee; staff helps tourists pick the right one and presentation is noted as 'delightful layered coffees.'
drink.quote: the delightful layered coffees

food.name: Guava and cheese pastry
food.reason: Specifically named and praised in a 5-star review as 'truly delicious' and 'not too sweet.' Only specific food item mentioned.
food.quote: The pastry was guava and cheese! Truly delicious.

reviewSample.text:
Nice place to sit and look out the window at people passing by.
And amazingly they figured out how to include tips in the prices. Probably one of the 5 places in the country that have this advanced technology. Love it.
Come at the right time and you will see freshly roasted coffee come out of the roaster.

We came here to try Cuban coffee. Never tried it! Miri was so sweet and helpful. She suggested the most popular drinks/ pastry and we were not disappointed. Our drinks were delicious! Pepe was kind enough to notice my husband’s coffee had not been stirred 😂 so thankfully my husband wasn’t bouncing off the walls after one sip. The pastry was guava and cheese! Truly delicious. Not too sweet. My kids had a mango smoothie and again not disappointed and not overly sweet. Definitely a place to visit! Great customer service. Good prices. Plenty of sitting space. Clean.

We went to this cafe for a Cuban coffee. The service was perfect, the atmosphere very nice and authentic and the coffee itself very good. But man, 11 $ for a coffee ? Was there gold in it ? I mean no coffee is worth 11$ for me no matter how good. A total scam. Maybe you can eat well but if you only want a coffee I think it’s better to go elsewhere.

drink.name: Café con leche
drink.reason: Authentic Cuban coffee is a signature draw, and café con leche is the specific drink reviewers order by name.
drink.quote: You want authentic Cuban coffee, this is the place

food.name: Havana Bowl
food.reason: Specifically named and praised as 'awesome' by a 5-star reviewer; other food mentions stay generic (pastries, sandwich, bacon).
food.quote: ordered a Cafe con leche, and a Havana Bowl!! Looked Awesome

reviewSample.text: This bakery open 24 hours/day is a must!  Awesome selection of pastries and cakes at great prices.  You want authentic Cuban coffee, this is the place.  Indoor outdoor seating.  Inside is old school at looks like Meyer Lansky and the gang could have been there.  lol

Went in the morning. It was busy. Staff was working hard and fast.

It was moderately fast.

I ordered a breakfast. I thought the cache con leche was included but I was charged extra for a cache but they asked what size and I said large. Maybe a small is included but the large is not, still
I was not informed of that.

Bacon was not visually appealing but tasted good. The bread was great. Felt full after.

Didn’t see the bathroom but the common area was beautiful

This place is sooo good! The food is amazing and super delicious. The staff are always smiling and super friendly. If you’re craving something really Latino, this is definitely the spot. Everything’s on point!

drink.name: Fresh juices
drink.reason: Reviewer specifically recommended fresh juices alongside coffee in a 5-star review; drinks generally praised as 'great' and 'visually appealing' across multiple reviews.
drink.quote: Try the croquetas trio, empanadas, their coffee and fresh juices

food.name: BBB pancakes
food.reason: Received the most enthusiastic, specific praise of any food item, called the best pancakes the reviewer ever had with highly detailed description.
food.quote: IT IS THE BEST I have ever had

reviewSample.text:
Taking the time to write an honest review about this place so others don’t find themselves in the same disappointment my family and I encountered.
The server was hyping up the chef for having 2 Michelin Stars but honestly the quality and taste of ALL our plates was firstly not representative of Cuban cuisine and also just overall unpleasant. I have never in my 25 years as a Cuban had such water down picadillo, are u kidding me? It was more like a chilli. Furthermore the tostones were soaked in oil, not crispy just soggy and drenched in oil. We also didn’t like the mango juice because it tasted like rotten green mangos. She kept using the excuse that “we don’t put sugar in our drinks” honey as someone who follows a strict no sugar diet I can tell u that the problem here was not the sugar and it was the quality of the mangos. Anyway I’ve never been met with such defensiveness before, usually when u don’t like a drink u can just get another one and not have to sit there and justify why the drink is unpleasant over and over again. A Cuban sandwich with an Italian baguette. The congriz, stale and hardly edible.
It really sucks when you spend so much money on a meal that was not enjoyable at all.. would not recommend this place unfortunately.

This place is super cute! Called 2 hours before on a Friday night & was able to make  a reservation for 4. It's not typical Cuban food- think more elevated,  specialized food. The space is super cute. Fair prices for the areas. Great drinks.The service is also absolutely phenomenal!! Ronaldo was our waiter and was great.

This place was DELICIOUS. Nany was our waitress and she made us feel like family and gave us great recommendations. Try the croquetas trio, empanadas, their coffee and fresh juices, oh and definitely do not skip desert! Rice pudding was incredible! We will be back!

drink.name: Latte
drink.reason: Specifically praised in a review and aligns with multiple mentions of excellent coffee, cappuccino, and the Shepherd Frappe. Latte got a direct callout tied to the café's name reputation.
drink.quote: I ordered their latte and it lived up to their name

food.name: Owner's cookies
food.reason: Described as 'pure heaven' in a detailed 5-star review highlighting the baked goods. Other food mentions are more general, making this the most specifically praised item.
food.quote: cookies made by the owner—oh my goodness, they were pure heaven

reviewSample.text:
I visited Shepherd Caffe on February 24, 2026, and it was such a wonderful experience. The cakes were absolutely delicious, and I ordered the cookies made by the owner—oh my goodness, they were pure heaven! I also tried the chocolate babka, and it was unbelievably good.
I was sitting outside and it felt amazing. (There is also indoor sitting )
Everything tasted amazing, the place was very, very clean, and the atmosphere was so positive with high vibes all around. You can truly feel the love and care put into the food and the space.
I highly recommend this place and can’t wait to come back again!

Great spot near the beach!
Found this place while staying nearby and I’m so glad I did. It’s just a block from Miami Beach and perfect for grabbing a coffee and walking to the ocean. The line was a bit long, but it moved fast. The food was great, coffee was smooth, and the staff were super friendly. Loved the cozy, Cuban-style vibe inside. We sat outside and enjoyed a relaxing breakfast in the Miami sunshine. Definitely recommend this place!

Shepherd Artisan Coffee in South Beach felt like old school meets modernity. The architecture and the interior design felt more classic. You can smell the fresh coffee as you step into the cafe which is almost always a good thing. I ordered their latte and it lived up to their name.

If you are in Miami, and are looking for a good cup of coffee, do not miss this place.

drink.reason: Specifically praised in a detailed 5-star review as decadent and perfectly served; matcha also mentioned positively but only once.

food.reason: Called the best ever in a 5-star review, and another review references their 'famous cookies,' giving this item the strongest food endorsement.

reviewSample.text: Genuinely the best chocolate cookie I’ve ever  had!! The matcha is amazing and they went above and beyond to make my birthday coffee so special! 10/10 if you’re in the design district and need a shopping break coffee and snack!

Tiny coffee & pastry spot in the lovely courtyard of the Design District mall.  Seating for approximately 6 people inside + approx 20 people outside.  Prices are fair.  Beautiful scene & very nice crowd.  The surrounding stores are uber-expensive & high end.

Walking into Maman in the Design District feels like being whisked away from the sleek, polished streets of Miami and dropped directly into a rustic garden in Southern France. The interior is nothing short of breathtaking—a whimsical blend of vintage wooden furniture, hanging greenery, and their signature blue-and-white floral patterns. It is an aesthetic triumph that feels warm, intentional, and deeply inviting, providing a soft contrast to the high-fashion industrialism of the surrounding neighborhood.
The vibe is effortlessly charming. It manages to be both a sophisticated meeting spot for the fashion elite and a cozy sanctuary for a quiet afternoon. There is a gentle hum of conversation that complements the curated decor, creating an atmosphere that is high-energy yet soothing. It is the type of space that feels like a "home away from home," provided your home is a chic, sun-drenched European cottage.
While many come for the famous cookies, the hot chocolate truly hits the spot. It is decadently creamy and served at the perfect temperature, offering a rich, comforting embrace in every sip. Whether you are seeking a visual feast for your social feed or a soul-warming treat, Maman delivers a quintessential Design District experience with a heartfelt, French-inspired soul.

drink.name: Hot chocolate
drink.reason: Multiple reviewers praise the chocolate sauce/paired chocolate; Americano was called mediocre.
drink.quote: perfectly paired with the chocolate

food.name: Churros
food.reason: Overwhelmingly praised across multiple 5-star reviews; reviewers returned specifically for them.
food.quote: Crispy on the outside, soft inside

reviewSample.text:
The churros here are absolutely amazing! It was so hard to find a place that serves churros filled with dulce de leche, and these were perfect ,the ideal size, incredibly flavorful, and freshly made. I loved everything about them. They truly reminded me of authentic Spanish-style churros.

I’m Brazilian, and we usually eat churros coated in sugar and cinnamon, so if they had that option it would have been even more perfect for me ,but honestly, I can’t describe how incredible they were. Simply delicious!

This shop is fantastic. It’s honestly a little sad that there’s nothing like this where I live, but every time I come back to Miami, I will definitely return just to get these churros again. Absolutely loved it!!!!!

This is an update at our second day visit - we loved the food so much so we decided to come again. But we waited for 25min with no food coming. We canceled our order and go to their neighbor instead.

This was the 1st visit: We had a wonderful brunch experience here with excellent customer service. The head chef was attentive, polite, and accommodating.

We had such a great experience at San Ginés Café! We tried their sandwiches, coffee, tea, and of course, churros — everything was excellent. The churros were so good that we actually came back the next day just for them! Crispy on the outside, soft inside, and perfectly paired with the chocolate. Cozy atmosphere, friendly service, and amazing food — definitely a must-visit spot if you love churros!

drink.name: Black coffee

drink.quote: a black coffee (the typical American watery style)

food.name: Smoked salmon omelet
food.reason: The only specifically named food item in the reviews, described positively as fresh and tasty alongside toast and potatoes.
food.quote: a smoked-salmon omelet with toast and potatoes... fresh and tasty

reviewSample.text:
🌷La Vie Belle is truly remarkable! My sibling and I enjoyed a later brunch in February as we visit Miami Beach annually.

We happened to stop by on a weekday and it was quiet so we got top notch service from the lovely staff. The atmosphere is so welcoming and pretty!

We order 3 big plates as we love to eat! Each one was amazing and we left so satisfied with our options!! It is a bit expensive but it might just be a reality of the current economy.

I will go again next year!!! ♥️

La Vie Belle truly lives up to its name — what a beautiful experience from start to finish! The aesthetic is absolutely stunning, every corner feels thoughtfully designed and perfect. The location is amazing and the staff made us feel so welcome with their friendly and attentive service.

The food was out of this world! Every dish was bursting with flavor and presented so beautifully. You can tell there’s real passion behind the menu. Everything was a 10/10 for flavor, presentation, and overall experience.

Highly recommend La Vie Belle if you’re looking for a spot that’s as gorgeous as it is delicious!

I loved the place! The hostess and our server Marco took great care of us. Their recommendations were very helpful, and their dedication to customer service, even taking pictures of us in such a beautiful setting, speaks volumes about the kind of people who work there. Thank you all, we will definitely be back to try more of your food ☺️

drink.name: Bottomless mimosas
drink.reason: Highlighted in a 5-star review as a great brunch deal at $25. Coffee also praised generically, but mimosas are the specific named drink with positive sentiment.
drink.quote: bottomless mimosas for just $25

food.name: Post-Apocalyptic Plate
food.reason: Explicitly called the highlight and a must-try in a 5-star review, with specific praise for its variety from salty to sweet.
food.quote: The highlight for me was the Post-Apocalyptic Plate

reviewSample.text:
This place doesn’t advertise loudly, but it’s truly a hidden gem. I want to start by giving a huge shoutout to Sonja. Her service and attention to the guests was outstanding. She made the whole experience even better.

The food is consistently delicious, and you can really taste the quality. Everything feels fresh and thoughtfully prepared. You can tell they genuinely care about the ingredients they’re using. I’ve ordered from here a few times before but this was my first time dining in and it definitely lived up to expectations.

Surprisingly, it wasn’t packed even though it was a holiday weekend, which made the experience even more enjoyable. And I didn’t grab a photo of the restroom, but it deserves 5 stars too. Clean bathrooms always matter. Gotta love that!

The parking situation might be a tad difficult because the best parking is on 8th st but so far i haven’t had an issue the times i’ve picked up food and this past time. All in all, i’ll be back!

(sorry about the half eaten food in the photos! we were starving! 😅)

I had a fantastic time at Craft! All of this deliciousness came at a great price — under $75 for 2 people— and if you’re into brunch drinks, they’ve got bottomless mimosas for just $25. It’s a really nice spot to relax, enjoy good food, and have a chill time.

What I really loved is how almost private it felt, with barely any crowd around.

The highlight for me was the Post-Apocalyptic Plate — It had everything from salty to swee. It’s a must-try if you love a bit of everything on your plate.

The sangria presentation was visually nice, though I’d say the flavor wasn’t all there, but it still had that savory edge that made it enjoyable.

I ordered two gorgeous breakfast plates, normally stacked with croissants, fruit, eggs, meat, avocado toast… the works. You know. Abundance. Brunch royalty.

Instead, I received… The Budget Cut Edition.

No receipt.
No utensils.
No napkins.

I politely asked. Was told, twice…“they’re in the bag.” The bag was stapled nicely though. Love the confidence. 🫶 Turns out the staples were the only thing holding this experience together.

After surviving 15 minutes of apocalyptic traffic, I opened the bag and discovered:

• One lonely poached egg for me
• Dark, suspicious-looking meat on both meals
• Croissants that had clearly gone through inflation… in reverse
• Portions so small I thought they accidentally sent the garnish tray
• Still no receipt, utensils, or napkins, so I guess I was supposed to eat with vibes

I literally had to combine four containers just to recreate one normal plate.

Photos 1 & 2 show what it usually looks like when dining in. Photo 3 shows what I received, aka Breakfast: The Mini-Series.

For $55, I expected brunch.
What I got was a tasting flight.

I wasn’t asking for extra, just what’s normally served when you sit inside the restaurant. Same menu. Same price. Apparently… half the food.

Would I return? Maybe…but only to dine in where the portions are supervised.

drink.name: Cafe Campesino (pour over)
drink.reason: The campesino/pour over coffee is the signature experience, poured tableside with storytelling about Colombian coffee culture. Multiple reviewers single it out as the highlight.
drink.quote: campesino coffee was poured tableside

food.name: Calentao de Origen
food.reason: Specifically named and described in detail in a 5-star review as a yummy traditional Colombian dish, with versions available for different diets. Jaguar salad and lentil soup also praised but each only once.
food.quote: Calentao de origen a yummy mixture of sweet plantain, corn, onion, beans

reviewSample.text: If you love great coffee, and are anywhere near Coral Gables, you should absolutely come here! An amazing coffee experience! Yes of course you can get espresso, cappuccino latte, macchiato, but the real excitement is in the pour over where you will have a choice of eight different flavor profiles from different Colombian growers. Delicious Colombian Food options too!  We had the Calentao de origen a yummy mixture Of sweet, plantain, corn, onion, beans, lentils, and eggs. They made a special vegetarian version for me, but you can get it with chorizo and bacon. The baked goods are also amazing,

One of the best unexpected brunches I’ve had in Miami! We strolled by and decided to stop in and were incredibly pleased with their ambiance, outstanding customer service, and delicious Colombian food! The campesino coffee was poured tableside and they even give you a story/background of the coffee culture in Colombia. We later found out they are a family run business and it makes me so happy to see places like this thrive. So many reasons to go try this place!! 100/10!

I found out about this colombian inpsired cafe through a barista who made excellent brews. Turns out he decided to go into business for himself and he opened up this cozy location. I drove almost an hour from Plantation to see how his attention to detail and quality carried over into his very own establishment. And he did not dissapoint. Their are trafitional homages to the most humble and iconic colombian dishes and coffees while having a unique twist on each one. And the quality of coffee here standa with any and all of the best cafes in miami without a doubt. Ill be back again and again

drink.name: Matcha

drink.quote: my favorite drink is the matcha

food.name: Garbanzo toast
food.reason: The only specifically named food item in the reviews, described as unique and memorable in a 5-star review.
food.quote: the garbanzo toast was unique and memorable

reviewSample.text:
Cosy unhurried place that can get a little noisy. Great for people watching the crowd vibing the Coral Gables fit. Food is outstanding. My matcha was well done and the garbanzo toast was unique and memorable. If I am back in this area, will surely stop by.

And of course, there are co-working spaces that's surely a draw for professionals. Networking with delicious bites, what's not to like?

I hope they have something like happy hours or beer nights for after hours socials. Depends on the license... But the place can surely expand to offer those experiences.

I had a wonderful experience visiting GROU Coffee + Cowork at Merrick Park with my mom. From the moment we arrived, the service was exceptional, and the atmosphere was warm and welcoming.

We were helped by the manager, Maru, and she truly made our visit memorable. She was extremely professional, helpful, and attentive. She took the time to explain the different menu items, as well as how the office and workspace areas function during the weekdays and the available parking options. Her thoroughness and willingness to help made us feel very comfortable and informed.

What stood out the most was seeing how dedicated she is to her work. You could tell she genuinely cares about the coffee shop and was working diligently to make sure everything was running perfectly. Her attention to detail, professionalism, and kindness are a true reflection of excellent leadership.

Overall, it was an amazing experience. The service, the environment, and the care put into everything make this place very special. Highly recommend, and I look forward to coming back!

I went to this coffee shop not really knowing what to expect. I knew it was also a coworking space, and I wanted to study and get some work done for a couple of hours. When I got there, it was pretty full, but luckily a table opened up quickly.
I ordered the mushroom coffee (chagaccino) with oat milk and agave, and honestly, it might be the best coffee I’ve ever had. The sweetness level was perfect, and the way the oat milk, agave, and coffee blended together was incredible. It didn’t feel heavy or too sweet, just a perfect mix of everything.
The vibes were great, the people were nice, and it was an awesome place to focus. Parking was easy to find when I went on a Monday around noon, though I’m sure weekends might be busier. I stayed for about two hours and really enjoyed the experience.
They also have a coworking space where you can rent private offices or areas to work, which is super convenient. I highly recommend this spot!

Service was excellent. Staff are friendly and helpful.

Atmosphere is perfect for a breakfast place.

Highly recommended.

Amazing food! Loved the portions person. This was a clean establishment for my first time visiting! The cookies are delicious and soft. The staff is polite and values the best experience. Definitely try the mocha coffee with espresso and oat milk!
Really good service, the food and coffee are amazing. It’s the best pit stop after a long ride — always my go-to spot. 🤟🏻

reviewSample.text:
Really nice coffee shop with great food options. The coffee is fantastic and there’s plenty to choose from. The food is just as good, I especially recommend the ratatouille on weekends, it was so good.

The overnight oats were also excellent. Prices were very fair, especially for the coffee. Can definitely recommend!

This cage was close to the hotel we stayed pre-cruise in miami. We went there for late lunch and also for coffee and pastries the next morning. I got the iced matcha with coconut milk and steak sub for lunch. The matching was pretty good but the sub was ok. The next day we got coffees and pastries and they were amazing. Really liked the vibe of the place and the food presentation was pretty good. The ambience of the Cafe was very modern. The wait staff were amazing. They remembered us when we went to the coffee from the day prior. On the whole I would recommend this place for morning coffee and pastries and a quick bite..

Gabriel was absolutely wonderful at Maman. I visited with two friends, and from the moment we arrived, he was attentive, kind, and genuinely made us all feel welcome. His service was thoughtful without being intrusive, and he paid great attention to every detail. He was knowledgeable about the menu, gave great recommendations, and made the whole experience feel relaxed and special. It’s clear he truly cares about providing excellent service. We’ll definitely be coming back—thank you, Gabriel, for such a great experience!

drink.name: Coffee

drink.quote: Loved the coffee

food.reason: Mentioned positively in two reviews including a 5-star, with specific notes about candied bacon and caramelized onion.
food.quote: I had the breakfast sandwich and it was good

reviewSample.text:
We came in around 5 p.m. hoping to get something to eat, only to be told the kitchen was already closed. Fine. Then I ordered a coffee with banana in it, exactly as listed on the menu. Apparently they didn’t have any bananas.

The coffee itself was good and the cake was very tasty. Unfortunately, that’s where the positive part ends.

The employee was extremely unfriendly and came across as very unsympathetic. No greeting, no “hello,” no basic courtesy at all. We basically had to push just to place an order. That is simply not good service. No matter how good the food or drinks may be, customer service is part of the experience—and here it was completely missing.

At 5:25 p.m., the signs from the street were brought inside and placed right next to our table, which gave the clear impression that we were supposed to leave.

That just added to the already unfriendly atmosphere and made us feel quite unwelcome.

Loved the coffee. The location is super convenient and a good atmosphere.
Maybe I came on a bad day but the food is really average. A bowl of potatoes with an egg on top and arugula was one of the choices. The potatoes were super salty. I took 2 bites and could not eat the rest. The waitress did not even ask… the breakfast sandwhich on a brioche was not bad. I’m surprised by the reviews.

Service: Adrian was excellent!!
We came on a cold windy day in Florida (go figure) and he offered us temporary seating and menus - he then made sure we got seated and serviced quickly. We ended up having an impromptu extra guest and he was very accommodating. I loved his fun and attentive attitude. Thank you Adrian!

Food:
The fresh squeezed orange juice was great! Papa’s breakfast bowl was tasty also.

drink.name: Latte
drink.reason: Multiple reviewers praised the lattes as smooth and flavorful, though one had a temperature complaint. Pour-over origins (Nicaragua) also received specific praise.
drink.quote: incredibly smooth and flavorful — easily some of the best

food.reason: No food items mentioned in any of the reviews.

reviewSample.text:
We stopped by Panther Coffee while passing through and were really impressed! We ordered a couple of lattes, and they were incredibly smooth and flavorful — easily some of the best we’ve had in a while.

The atmosphere is rustic yet inviting, with a warm local vibe that makes you want to stay awhile. Indoor seating is a bit limited, but the overall experience more than makes up for it.

If we lived in the area, we’d definitely be regulars. Highly recommend stopping in for a quality cup of coffee and great service!

We tried the vanilla cream nitro cold brew and found it a bit sour and not as smooth as expected. The latte was also disappointing—the milk temperature wasn’t well controlled, with the bottom being too hot, so you definitely need to be careful not to get burned. Two coffees came out to $20, which felt pricey for the quality. Not sure if it’s just Miami prices or this location, but overall it wasn’t worth the hype for us.
High quality specialty coffee, with V60 and Chemex. They have different coffee origins with great tasting notes (Nicaragua and Ethiopia recommended)

food.quote: We got the croissant with jamón y queso

reviewSample.text:
A coffee shop you should definitely visit. The staff is incredibly helpful. I think it's quite difficult to find good cold brew in Miami. They usually serve filtered coffee cold. It needs to be distilled. For black cold brew lovers, I definitely recommend checking it out. The coffee has a non-acidic flavor. It has a small and chill atmosphere. I also liked the music playlist. You're great.
Thanx 🤘🏻

Stopped by for a quick Cafecito, and was absolutely blown away. It was absolutely delicious and on par with some of the best espresso I've had in Italy. The owner was kind, chatted with us and recommended a few spots to check out in the are. You can tell he is passionate about coffee and makes each cup special. Don't miss out!

Havana Café is a cozy, laid-back spot that’s perfect if you’re looking for a relaxed atmosphere. The coffee was truly excellent—definitely a highlight! While they don’t have a huge food selection, that minimalist charm fits the vibe. The service was on the slower side since there was only one person working, but it felt like part of the easygoing, unhurried ambiance. If you’re not in a rush and want a great cup of coffee, this place has a charm all its own.

reviewSample.text: Maman Coral Gables  is a beautiful lunch cafe, gourmet coffee shop and pattiserie.  I had the Croque "maman" with absolutely delicious homemade bechemel sour dough, ham and fried egg.  Brother had excellent ham and cheese on everything croissant w incredible "layered circle" presentation. Ice coffee hit the spot.  Next time have to try the matcha affagato and chocolate pistachio croissant. Ashley Sophia's great service and smile made it an extra special experience.  Chk it.

Thank you, Ashley for serving me!
I have been to another location not too far from this area so I headed here for my morning Matcha because I knew it would have a beautiful atmosphere on par with service!
I ordered a hot Matcha latte and an iced Sparkling Turmeric Ginger & Thyme Macha Tonic Fall Special to go.
The Fall Special Matcha Tonic has a healthy immune boosting tangy flavor, which I love.
I highly recommend this place.
This particular location is sweet with a warm family vibe. It’s perfect for a Sunday brunch.

Maman never misses. I've been to both the Coral Gables and Brickell locations, and the food and drinks are amazing at both. The best part is the service. The Coral Gables location is so cozy. I stopped by on a Saturday morning for breakfast. It was busy but I was still seated and served quickly. Staff are friendly, attentive and fast. Thanks Eduardo for your recommendations and great service!

We came here for my daughter’s 21st birthday celebration and had a great time. We will definitely be back!

I stopped by this beautiful French café in Miami Beach after the establishment caught my attention, and I’m so glad I did! The food was delicious and the coffee was honestly 10/10. Perfect spot for a quick bite and coffee break. The service was amazing as well — the hostess was super kind, and Ashley and Washington took great care of me. They were both so personable and welcoming. You can really tell they care about the customer experience. I would definitely come back and highly recommend checking this place out!

Our whole experience here was excellent, the service, the food, the drinks. I came here to get the picture on my coffee and it’s surprisingly only two dollars extra what a deal! It was a pretty cool experience. I love the ambience and it was just to walk away from our hotel/three minutes. Ashley was wonderful made recommendations and explained everything. Everyone made our experience amazing, we will be back! This place is a MUST to anyone thinking of coming.. DO IT! I had the avocado toast and my husband had the French toast. Everything was absolutely delicious. I had a beat smoothie and a beat cocktail. The food was cooked healthy with no added sugars or salt for my health concensous people :)

reviewSample.text: I came with my mom for the first time today on National women’s day and I was not disappointed by Novela Café Social, I was impressed. My mom and I love it, she got the Spanish breakfast and I got the avocado toast with eggs and we both got matcha latte. Best place to get a matcha latte. The place is super calm, welcoming with a peaceful atmosphere and the vibe is great. Highly recommended!

Novela is a smart choice if you want to spend the day in Wynwood. The location is excellent—just four blocks from Wynwood Walls—making the walk effortless. The patio is lovely and has that cozy, backyard-at-home feeling, with enough space between tables to actually relax.

It’s also dog-friendly, which is a huge plus for pet owners like me; the pups add to the warm, homey atmosphere.

The food is decent—not remarkable—but the prices are very fair, so it feels balanced. Same goes for the service: nothing extraordinary, but good enough for a casual, pleasant outing.

Overall, it’s a solid pick for brunch or a girls’ day out. I’d definitely come back.

We had a great breakfast experience here. The place is very nice.

Hellen’s service was excellent! very friendly and attentive at all times.

The food was delicious, well presented, and full of flavor.

We would definitely come back. Highly recommended!

reviewSample.text: Was very excited to try this new patisserie in wynwood. The desserts were beautiful and tasted wonderful. They didnt have any really great seasonal latte options which i would like to see in the future. The truffle fries were bomb! Will def be back to try other items. The only disappointment was the service was not good. We had to keep reminding our service to bring us things like napkins, water, and drinks that we ordered. Not sure if they need more servers or what.

Loved this restaurant! The vibe upon entering was very welcoming and the service was friendly. To start we had the fish burger and fries which was delicious (!!!) and the tuna toast wasabi. I would definitely go back and order those again. Every bite was packed with flavor.

Of course we had to get desserts. We wanted to try the cheesecake matcha and the croissant but unfortunately those were sold out. Instead we had galette des rois, strawberry French cake, pineapple cake, and the brioche with pearl sugar. Everything was delicious and it was obvious that they were thoughtfully made. We met the pastry chef and we learned that he used to be a pastry chef at Dominique Ansel in NYC - which I am a big fan of!

10/10 would come back when I’m in Miami again✨

We had an incredible experience at this restaurant! The atmosphere was warm and welcoming from the moment we walked in. The food was absolutely delicious  every dish was beautifully presented and full of flavor. But what truly made our visit special was the service. Nicolas, our waiter, was amazing attentive, friendly, and genuinely made sure we had the best experience possible.

And the desserts… wow! Each one was perfection  rich, fresh, and beautifully crafted. You can tell a lot of love and detail goes into their menu. We left completely satisfied and can’t wait to come back. Highly recommend this place for anyone looking for excellent food, top-notch service, and unforgettable desserts!

reviewSample.text: Studio 24 is such a vibe. The space is modern, warm, and thoughtfully designed, and the energy feels creative the moment you walk in. The drinks are not only beautiful but so well balanced and flavorful—you can tell real care goes into every cup. The staff is friendly, efficient, and clearly passionate about what they do. reviewSample.text: Treat yourself and escape your daily routine here! I highly recommend the matcha and the Basque cheesecake. The atmosphere is great, and the manager, Charlie, is incredibly kind and gentle with every guest. Such a welcoming spot!

I was really looking forward to trying this place after hearing so many great things. Unfortunately, I was a bit disappointed. The cachito wasn’t served at the right temperature… the inside was cold even after being warmed in the oven, which made me question its freshness.

The coffee was good, and the service was friendly. We especially enjoyed the yuca bread bites. The location, however, is quite small and can feel overwhelming when it’s full, especially considering that dogs are welcomed. I also wish the space was larger as I believe it will make it more welcoming. Parking in the area is difficult to find.

😋😋😋😋😋
I've been a loyal fan of Magdalene Coffee in Wynwood since they opened, and it's consistently one of my favorite spots! Their coffee is always fantastic, brewed to perfection every time. You absolutely have to try their Danish pastries—they're incredibly delicious and taste so fresh. It's the perfect treat to go with your coffee.
I also love their hours, open from 7 AM to 7 PM, which makes it easy to grab a great cup whenever you need it. And if that wasn't enough, they gift their clients with beautiful flowers, a truly lovely touch that makes every visit special. Plus, if you want to enjoy their amazing coffee at home, you can buy their beans and have them ground right there for you to take home. Magdalene Coffee truly stands out!

reviewSample.text:
They have unique flavors like salted caramel, and tiramisu latte, which brought is here to their location.

Drinks were crafted and balanced really well. They informed us that they use heavy cream or some of the drinks we ordered. We generally get all our drinks with oat milk and can do a little bit of dairy, but this amount of Dairy didn’t agree with us, but that’s on us because we were given fair warning about the heavy cream-something to be mindful of.

They also seemed to have some good pastries, but we didn’t have any room in our tummy for those.

Good place to visit if you’re looking for refined and special flavors.

Beautiful experience in the place, a super warm bar with very good energy. I can confirm that one of the best in Miami Beach.
The coffee is delicious, one of my favorites in the place. Delfina is divine, super loving and her attention is unique. I fell in love with the bar, its people and the beautiful attention.

I finally found a place in South Beach that understands coffee and equals my passion. XO Espresso Bar is not your big coffee chain. They roast on site. They serve coffee in real cups and glasses. Their pastries and baked goods are fresh. The only downside is that it’s inside a food court, which makes some hard to find but once you do find them, you’ll keep coming back.

drink.name: Lavender Cappuccino

food.name: Avocado Toast
food.reason: Praised in two separate 5-star reviews for its texture, authenticity, and thoughtful preparation with soft boiled egg and seed seasoning.
food.quote: super satisfying crunch and texture, like a real Australian brunch plate

reviewSample.text:
Surry Hills was so memorable I barely captured any photos or video, I just wanted to be in it. The space is calm and confident, and you can feel how intentional everything is, inside and out. Even the tiny things are dialed in, like the soap and lotion in the bathroom, and the specialty dog treats that make it feel genuinely welcoming. They’ve been recognized by The World’s 100 Best Coffee Shops, and once you sit down, you get why.  ￼

Now the food and coffee. Their coffee is perfectly dialed in, smooth, balanced, and consistent, the kind of flat white that tastes like someone actually cared about ratio, texture, and temperature, not just speed.  ￼The almond croissant was flawless, soft and gooey inside, with that crunchy almond top that gives you the perfect snap in every bite.  ￼

And then the savory stuff surprised me in the best way. The avocado toast has that super satisfying crunch and texture, and it tastes like a real Australian brunch plate, not the watered down version. The quinoa salad with chicken was a wow too, hearty but still fresh, with edamame, greens, seeds, and that dukkah vibe that makes it feel intentional instead of just “healthy.”  ￼They got it all right, the coffee, the food, the details, and the energy.

More on social media: @ratedbymaryam

One of my go-to coffee shops/ cafe in Miami. I love that their coffee is strong yet well balanced. Their food is plated beautifully and tastes phenomenal and fresh. Everyone that works there is kind and helpful. I enjoy that they often change their menu by highlighting new and different drinks/plates.

First visit here and thoroughly enjoyed the ambiance, service and food.

Met the owner, Francisco and had a nice convo on the other locations in Argentina and Spain.

The lavender cappuccino was perfect.

The tuna melt was very good!

The turkey croissant with lime was scrumptious.

Looking forward to our next visit.

2nd Visit: enjoyed a lavender/vanilla cappuccino and the egg/sausage sandwich. Both excellent!

3rd visit: Enjoyed the protein bowl and a lavender/vanilla cappuccino. The only issue with this place is they don't have a location within walking distance from my home 😅.

reviewSample.text:
Great food and even better service! We went to the Threefold Cafe for brunch on a Sunday and it was packed! We got the Okonomiyaki with the salmon, the french toast with strawberries and cream, and fries. The fries were crispy and great. The French Toast was stunning and the lemon anglaise was yummy.
The staff were very kind and got our food out swiftly. Highly recommend!

I stopped by around 9 a.m. to have breakfast outside and it was just what I needed to get the day started.

The cappuccino was delicious, so I grabbed another. The waitress recommended the "Brekke Sengge" (I'm totally spelling this wrong), and it was INCREDIBLE! The sourdough had a crunch with a soft middle. The sauces makes the flavors pop, and the crispy bacon gave it that wonderful crunch.

Did I mention the cappuccino was delicious? ☕

Highly recommend Threefold Cafe 🙌🏽
Best spot we patroned while in Miami. Food was excellent, staff was phenomenal, and the vibe was great! Can't recommend enough. Make this a stop on your visit.

Pasion del Cielo is an absolutely charming coffee shop. The service is warm and friendly from the moment you walk in, and the atmosphere invites you to stay. I ordered a low-acid, smooth coffee with coconut milk, paired with a coconut cake — and it was the perfect combination! Everything was delicious. Watching them grind the coffee beans right in front of you makes the experience even more special. A perfect spot to enjoy high-quality coffee and a relaxing moment.

reviewSample.text:
Libertino Café in Wynwood has an incredible vibe that’s hard to beat. The space is stylish and welcoming, with plenty of big communal tables where you can sit with your laptop to work, study, or just hang out. There’s no long line or hectic rush, which makes it an especially relaxing spot to spend time. Vibe: 100/10.

As for the food and drinks, they’re okay but not the main draw. We ordered the mocha and the matcha, and while neither was terrible, they weren’t our favorites. The food itself was average—nothing bad, just not particularly memorable. Prices are fair for the area, and a nice bonus is that you can use the InKind app for payment, which makes it even more convenient.
Update: this place has a horrible service, the first time I came it wasn’t great but the second literally is making me not come again, the place gets very packed and the person that was supposed to be in charged of the register was very rude and refused to take our order because they were “packed”. This place could be great with a better service. Not coming again!
Overall, Libertino is definitely a place to chill, meet friends, or get some work done while enjoying the cool Wynwood atmosphere. It’s worth visiting at least once for the vibe alone, but the drinks and food probably won’t have you rushing back. A solid hangout spot, just not necessarily a repeat for the menu.

The food is mediocre, more expensive than it needs to be for the quality you receive. You are automatically charged a 12% service fee which is absolutely ridiculous considering you 1. order from a self serve kiosk/tablet 2. have to pick up your food yourself and bring it to your own table and 3. aren't even greeted when you walk in the door. Because of the chaos and lack of clear systems in place, during my second and most recent visit, another customer mistakenly collected our drink order, brought it to their table and began to consume said drinks. Upon realizing they took the wrong order, they brought it back to the counter. We inquired about this mishap to the barista to have our drinks remade, she pointed at the returned drinks implying that we take the already touched/consumed drinks and when we explained that we did not want to do so, she remade our drinks but with an attitude. The space is great for working though and the ube matcha I had my first time was good.

I recently went to Libertino Cafe and tried their strawberry matcha—it was so good! I also ordered the tomato toast, which was fresh and delicious. I went on a Saturday and it was really crowded, to the point there were no seats available, but despite that, I really enjoyed their breakfast. Definitely a spot worth visiting!

A super cool new coffee location right in the middle of Brickell! Really nice atmosphere and amazing drinks! Have been coming everyday since they have opened, whether to work or just sit and relax!

reviewSample.text:
The space is beautiful, you feel like you're in a secret garden. But the good things end there. We ordered a cappuccino, which was already at $8. And a cortado for $6. The cappucino didn't look like your typical cappucino, and it didn't taste very good either, it tasted watered down. The cortado looked like what you would expect for a cappuccino, but unfortunately it was too hot and the espresso tasted burnt. I was very disappointed since they advertised themselves as a coffee roaster place.

We ordered an avocado toast with smoked salmon for an eye popping $28. It was pretty basic. We paid for $55 with tips for 3 items. For the money we paid, and the value, it was overall just disappointing.

You can find better coffee and food elsewhere.

This charming and cozy spot in the South Beach area is a great find! The menu offers a well-balanced selection—enough variety to satisfy different tastes without feeling overwhelming.

The only small drawback is the coffee, which could use a slight improvement. I’m quite particular about coffee, so that might just be me, but overall, it’s a lovely place worth visiting!

I wanted to like Salon du Cafe. It has a great ambiance and a nice setup, even if it’s a bit overpriced (miami beach).

Unfortunately, the coffee was truly terrible. I ordered a cortado and it was, without exaggeration, the worst cup of coffee I’ve ever had - bitter, burnt, and almost tasted spoiled. I’ve been drinking coffee for decades but this one will forever stand out in my memory as the worst coffee I’ve ever had.

The place has potential, but they  need to fix their coffee.

Alongside it, I indulged in a chocolate croissant, and it was just delightful—flaky, decadent, and perfectly sweet. If you’re a coffee lover, this place is a must-visit!

reviewSample.text:
Such a lovely little breakfast spot in the area! They have a great selection of pastries—everything looked (and tasted) amazing—and their coffee was delicious. They also offer focaccias, which looked incredible, but we didn’t get a chance to try them this time.

We went on a Thursday morning and it was already pretty busy, so I can only imagine how packed it gets on the weekends. It’s definitely a small space with limited seating, so plan accordingly. But if you’re craving good coffee, sweet treats, and cozy vibes, it’s absolutely worth the stop.

It has taken me a long time to come to terms with the fact this is best bakery in the united states. I am a bakery lover. I have been to countless bakeries around the country and world to find the best and how lucky am I to have found the best bakery in the country a few miles away from my home (am i lucky or does the universe just want me to gain weight?). The croissants are the best I’ve ever had. I don’t know what they put in the dough, but it is phenomenal. I went over the weekend and they had cardamom buns which I had been looking forward to for weeks because there is no cardamom buns in the United States that are worth having quite frankly. Now these are worth having. I wanted to gate keep so bad but I can’t anymore. Baker and Barista deserves all the hype and more.

Baker and Barista is a gem in every sense of the word!!!! Nestled towards the latter end of Design District- I recommend parking at paradise plaza and walking over because it's well worth it!

The vanilla latte was excellent, as well as the smores drink! My highlight was the amazing olive oil cake- wooooooooooooooow.

The team was so friendly and welcoming- this place is a staple and you can tell everything is made with love!!!

We need more places like this in Miami. Baker and Barista, Godspeed ahead!!!! Thank you for the awesome experience.

drink.name: Italian-style coffee
drink.reason: Praised in 5-star review as rich and freshly made to order; espresso macchiato and iced latte received criticism (bitter/weak), so the general Italian-style coffee is the safer pick.
drink.quote: coffee is also excellent, with a rich flavor and an Italian-style touch

food.quote: croissants are beautifully baked—light yet sturdy

reviewSample.text:
The barista clearly knows what he’s doing and takes pride in making drinks well. Unfortunately, my espresso macchiato missed the mark—it was strong, which I liked, but also noticeably bitter and acidic, which made it hard to enjoy. It felt like the beans or machine might need some adjustment.

This is a very small shop with no bathroom. They offer a few basic food items like a ham and cheese sandwich, croissants, and empanadas. They’re also advertising a “Matcha Cloud,” which sounded interesting, though I didn’t try it.

Overall, it just didn’t stand out enough for me to return—especially with so many better coffee options nearby.

The restaurant is exceptionally well located, less than a block from the beach and just steps away from Ocean Drive. The food has an excellent, naturally fresh flavor that really stands out. The croissants are beautifully baked—light yet sturdy—and the empanadas have that comforting, homemade taste that feels like something prepared with real care. The coffee is also excellent, with a rich flavor and an Italian-style touch that’s freshly made to order. The atmosphere is relaxed, the service is friendly, and overall it’s a great spot to enjoy a meal close to the ocean.

I especially want to thank Vicky, who took care of us during our visit. She was exceptionally kind, attentive, and made us feel truly welcome throughout the entire experience. Highly recommended.

A Beautiful little Caffe on Ocean drive. Definitely check reviews before you shop or buy any food. This place came up near our hotel and we loved it. Cute, dante place, great service too. Most definitely recommend for a breakfast spot to start your day. Thank you for the Dante Cafe experience. 💫

drink.name: Nitro cold brew
drink.reason: Specifically praised in a 5-star review as beautifully layered and flavorful. Other drinks (Sage latte, Miami Fog, iced latte) each only mentioned once, with the Sage latte criticized as too sweet and overpriced.
drink.quote: beautifully layered and full of flavor

food.reason: Only one review briefly mentions food (mushroom empanadas and gluten-free cookies) without specific praise beyond calling them 'solid vegan snacks.' Not enough data to confidently recommend.

reviewSample.text: Enjoyed the Sage latte with date cold cream. Such a great drink, a little too sweet but very unique and you could taste all the different flavors. So expensive. 13$ for a coffee… wild. I’d rather just taste the beans but while in Miami, I guess I should try something cool 😝

VIBRANT SPACE & SERIOUSLY GOOD COFFEE

Dropped by Panther Coffee in Coconut Grove, and it absolutely lived up to the hype. The space itself is fantastic. It is warm, modern, and thoughtfully designed with beautiful wood finishes and a great energy. It was buzzing with people working, chatting, and enjoying the vibe.

I ordered their nitro (pictured) and it was beautifully layered and full of flavor. The menu is deep, with a mix of single-origin espresso options, cold brews, teas, and even a full lemonade and coconut bar. Whether you’re a purist or just want something refreshing and fun, they’ve got you covered.

The service was friendly and quick, and the entire setup; from the register flow to the outdoor seating—felt effortless. Also, the branded cup and neon panther sign? Chef’s kiss.

This place is a must if you’re in Coconut Grove. Whether you’re a local or passing through Miami, Panther Coffee nails the experience for both coffee lovers and casual visitors alike.

Great coffee shop in the heart of Coconut Grove. The vibe is super chill and minimalist, with a tropical feel—perfect for working, meeting up with friends, or just relaxing solo.

Coffee is excellent. I had an iced latte with macadamia milk and it was smooth and rich. They also have cold brew, espresso flights, and some solid vegan snacks (like mushroom empanadas and gluten-free cookies).

Prices are on the higher side (my latte was around $8), and the service can be a little slow during busy times. But overall, totally worth it if you’re into specialty coffee and good ambiance.

Will definitely be back.☕🌴

reviewSample.text: This place is INCREDIBLE!! The owners are so nice and every drink we had was 10/10. Would highly recommend to anyone wanting coffee, matcha or chai tea! We might come back here everyday of our trip. SO good!
Incredible service. High quality ingredients and techniques. Very knowledgeable staff. Delicious horchata latte. A little pricy ($9 for mostly ice) but great atmosphere makes it worth it.

Incredible coffee… but my favorite was a iced matcha latte with their pistachio milk. One of the best coffee spots in Miami with a neighborhood/family feel. The best duo MaFe and Jorge, thanks for las atenciones!

FYI - if you drive, park at the lot outside and walk in through the gates.

drink.name: Cold brew

reviewSample.text:
Honestly really disappointed with my experience. The food took almost 40 minutes to come out, and for the price, I expected much better quality and portion sizes. If a bowl is going to cost around $20, it should at least feel worth it.

On top of the long wait, the food itself was pretty underwhelming, and unfortunately there was even a hair in my meal, which completely ruined the experience for me. I normally don’t leave reviews, but this was genuinely frustrating. I hope the restaurant works on improving both food quality and service times moving forward. Btw this is my second ever review I am leaving and the other one might have been a worse place than this one which is wild. Weird thing is they didn’t refund me for the food they gave me a cookie instead 😭.

Honestly very disappointed this time. I usually go to different Crema Gourmet locations and never had any issues before, but this was my first time trying this one and it was one of my worst experiences. The Caesar salad had more dressing than anything else, and the dressing was extremely salty to the point of being inedible. The matcha tasted like milk and water with barely any matcha flavor. Honestly, for the price and reputation of the place, this is shameful.

The eggs were fine, nothing special. 1 jelly and 1 butter was kinda stingy. The tuna salad was a generous portion but it had no dressing on the salad. I encouraged my fiancé to ask for some because there was a lot of salad that was completely dry. The waitress seemed very surprised and a bit put out that he actually asked for dressing for a salad. She asked "what kind?", balsamic vinaigrette, "white balsamic or regular?" She came back with a small container of dressing and said that the kitchen said "it doesn't come with dressing". Yeah obviously, but it was needed and politely requested. It was more of an issue than it should have been. He said it tasted much better with the dressing. We would go back because he loves a good tuna salad, but next time he'd order it with dressing, hopefully receiving it without an issue or a minor guilt trip.

drink.name: Espresso
drink.reason: Multiple reviewers praise the authentic, high-quality coffee; the espresso received the most detailed and enthusiastic praise.
drink.quote: rich, creamy, and perfectly balanced

food.name: Croissant

reviewSample.text:
I stumbled upon Umbria Cafe early in the morning during my run in South Beach after realizing I was out of coffee at home. Just a few blocks from my place, I found this cozy spot and ordered a single espresso. The gentleman asked if it was for here or to go. I said for here. To my surprise, the espresso was served in a proper porcelain cup with a small glass of sparkling water. I was shocked.

I have visited countless coffee shops across the U.S., including the so-called fancy ones like Starbucks, and none have ever served espresso the authentic Italian way with water on the side. That simple gesture instantly transported me back to Italy in Rome, Milan, or Florence, where espresso is treated like an art form.

The coffee itself was rich, creamy, and perfectly balanced. The sparkling water added the perfect touch, refreshing and ideal for cleansing the palate after a delicious espresso. That moment alone earned this café five stars.

If you appreciate true coffee culture, Umbria Cafe is a gem worth visiting.
I got the coconut cloud iced coffee and it was really good!  My sister had the chicken empanadas and croissants.  She enjoyed her breakfast.  Outdoor seating was nice.

I love Caffe Umbria for its design and unique menu! Today I visited it and drank Oat Meal Latte. It reminded me of great spring time I spent in Portland, Oregon where I tried this coffee for the first time. Thank you

reviewSample.text:
Got the cuban oat milk latte as recommended by the barista. It has a special sweetener in it that made it cuban style. Tasted great.

Spinach and cheese empanada was crispy on the outside and the fillings were cheesy with spinach. Not too heavy for breakfast.

Highly recommended to give this coffee shop a go if you happen to be around the area.

Cute little coffee/ baked goods truck. Staff were excellent. I got a strawberry matcha. I would give it a 3/5. The colour was really good but the matcha was a bit bitter for my liking. Cute spot to stop by on a walk.
CJ and Elide are wonderful! We have never been to Miami before and they were so helpful! I had the Cuban oat milk iced Latte and my hubby got the tiramisu latte both were amazing! Highly recommend.

drink.reason: Multiple reviewers highlight the in-house tres leches milk as a standout, with one calling the pumpkin tres leches 'amazing' and another raving about it added to an americano.
drink.quote: the pumpkin tres leches latte was amazing

reviewSample.text:
Impressed.

Everyone says to get the tres leches latte - but that’s too much milk/sugar for me.

The barista was super friendly and helpful, she explained that the tres leches milk they actually make in-house (it’s not just syrups).

So, she made me an iced americano, and instead of me putting in some cream, she put in a little bit of the tres leches milk. WOW. Very very good, not too sweet, but you can definitely taste the sweetness and even a little coconut.

I will 100% be back for this!!

Highly recommend asking for the tres leches milk added in small part to your basic coffee order.

First time checking out this spot in the neighborhood and it did not disappoint! I had the Chai Latte and my wife got the Caramel Latte both were delicious with awesome flavor. Super friendly vibe, great service, and definitely a new go to for our weekend coffee runs!

Love this place!!! Very cozy, friendly atmosphere and coffee is exceptional! Amazing way to start your day and it is a real treat!!! Parking may be a challenge but it is so worth it. They don’t have a lot of sitting areas bec the concept is to go but we always stay and hang around. Would love for owner to add throw pillows around for clients to sit and hang around:-) but coffee is a killer and flavors are sincerely good … Must try, must go to place doesn’t matter what time of the day

reviewSample.text: Sunshine, waterfront vibes and music set the tone as iced chai latte and iced latte were just the job. Smoked salmon bagel and breakfast bagel hit big; fresh, punchy and lightly toasted, perfect. The way that sunny side yolk ran through the crispy bacon & arugula, layered over the cream cheese & peach jam, oh yes! Outdoor seating, Miami in motion. Abdul runs this place, he’s the man, great service and good energy. High spot for tea, coffee, cool beverages and brunch classics, with a view. Love it.

I ordered the frappe cappuccino, which was good, but I could barely taste the coffee. The atmosphere is really nice, but it's a bit hard to hear when they call your order. They should consider using a microphone or lowering the music a little.

I definitely want to come back to try their croissants and other food.

If you’re looking for a place to take a walk and grab something healthy to eat, I definitely recommend this spot. It has a beautiful view, delicious food, and a relaxing atmosphere. With seaside tables and chairs, it’s a peaceful place that suits all ages and is surrounded by nature. The calorie values weren’t listed on the menu, which would’ve been nice, but the coffee was tasty — just average overall

reviewSample.text: Stopped by this coffee shop because we had a long drive ahead of us from Miami, FL back home, & we are coffee enthusiasts. Out of all the coffee shops I’ve been to, this is in my top two. My buddy told me this is genuine Cuban coffee, & it is! The location is amazing, & the shop is well-decorated & organized. They had multiple food items & lots of coffee options. I forgot exactly what I ordered, but it was fresh, cold, &—most of all—tasty! The staff were friendly, helpful, & professional.
I could say it really good for first time to taste Cuba coffee. I’ve told the staff that I cannot drink too sweet coffee please less stweet. Then I gotta a perfect one!!! It’s sooooo good!

I want to recognize Oscar, Dalila, and Gipsy for their excellent customer service. They are consistently friendly, attentive, and make every visit a great experience. Their professionalism truly stands out. My matcha 11/10

drink.name: Matcha latte
drink.reason: Multiple reviewers ordered matcha; one calls it the best in Miami, another loved the customizable sweetness, and a third found it good though slightly bitter.

reviewSample.text: Living in Key West, I have been coming to Miami weekly for medical care for over a year and I discovered Navi towards the beginning of 2025 and it’s easily become one of my favorite go-to’s in Miami and a place that has made these trips worth looking forward to. Not only are their lattes delicious, they have amazing pastries and it’s always my lucky day when they have their pistachio croissants. They have a cozy and small aesthetic with fresh florals always available, but what has made this spot so special to me is the treatment from their baristas and owners. They have always been so warm and welcoming. Navi recently made floral arrangements for us to give to our surgeons and they were the most beautiful, whimsical bohemian style arrangements that I could tell were made with so much love. I will be coming here for all of my Miami floral needs from here on out. You can’t go wrong with a stop at Navi!

The coffee shop is really cute, with a cozy and nice vibe ✨. The service was great☺️, the person at the register was super friendly. I ordered a classic matcha with almond milk and cinnamon, no sweeteners. The matcha was good, though a bit bitter, it might be a mix between culinary and ceremonial grade matcha. Overall, I liked it and would come back💖. It was around $9 for the matcha. If you’re a matcha lover, I’d say it’s worth trying, even though it’s not the best I’ve ever had.

Delightful little cafe/coffee shop. I had a wonderful iced latte and the barista was so friendly and helpful when explaining the drink options. They had some tasty looking pastries, but I was in a bit of a hurry and didn’t have the opportunity to try them. Such a clean aesthetic and bright and airy interior. There is a good amount of seating outside too which is nice and most of those seats are shaded. They also sell beautiful arrangements of flowers :)
