reviewSample.text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Perfect spot for coffee and work! ☕💻
Excellent coffee and a very welcoming atmosphere. The staff is friendly, and the WiFi is great, making it the perfect place to get some work done. Highly recommended if you're looking for quality coffee and a productive environment!

Black Sheep coffee around is a must go. We came with our toddler and dog and had a good time. There’s enough space inside to sit with your dog and they are pet friendly. The baby was able to move around. The food was fresh. And the coffee and Matcha are out of this world. If you’re in the area, I highly recommend you stop by.

I have been to Black Sheep Coffee several times, and it has become our family’s favorite coffee shop. The staff is patient, helpful, and kind. My wife and youngest son, who has autism, both love the coffee and the chocolate chip cookies.

The ambience is uplifting, the seating is cozy and comfortable, and the eclectic clientele is part of what makes having coffee in Coconut Grove such a delight. As a former Starbucks barista, I know what it is like on the other side of the counter, and what sets Black Sheep Coffee apart is the coffee, the service, and the genuine sense of community.

It feels like a true "third place," meaning a welcoming space outside home and work where people can gather, connect, and feel comfortable staying awhile. Sociologist Ray Oldenburg popularized that idea, and some 25 years ago he asked me to write a chapter in his book *Celebrating the Third Place*, so the concept has special meaning for me.

I am a disabled senior, and I was impressed enough to even download the app. I also appreciate that the facilities are ADA accessible. I use a walker and get about with ease, which makes the whole experience feel even more welcoming and inclusive.

The location is especially convenient if you take the train. You can hop off, grab a coffee, and be on your way. The general manager, Abner, is attentive and professional, and the place reminds me a bit of Uncommon Grounds, a coffee shop I used to visit daily in Berkeley. In my opinion, Black Sheep Coffee is one of the few places in South Florida that truly feels like a real neighborhood coffee shop.

reviewSample.text: Super chill, lounge vibes, and that barista Jose, one of the greatest energy I have came across. Looks like he loves what he does and is always smiling with every customer. Very very helpful guy and knows how to tailor coffee to your liking with just a brief description. The French toast latte for the morning and the MIAMI SUNSET for the evening. This coffee shop is amazing. And that's just the coffee shop. Wait til you find out about the mission behind their profits.

Corner Coffee might be one of the best hidden gems in Downtown Miami. Perfectly located next to The Elser, it’s the kind of place you walk into for a quick coffee and end up wanting to stay for hours. The coffee is genuinely incredible — smooth, rich, and crafted with care. Easily one of my favorite cups in the city.

But what makes this place truly special is the mission behind it. Corner Coffee is connected to First Church Miami, an institution with deep roots in the community and a longstanding commitment to serving others. For decades, they’ve operated the Breakfast Club, providing breakfast and showers to neighbors experiencing homelessness in Overtown. That spirit of compassion and service is woven into the culture of the café itself.

In a city full of trendy coffee spots, Corner Coffee stands out because it offers both excellence and purpose. Great coffee, warm people, and a mission that actually matters. Miami needs more places like this.

Amazing coffee shop in Miami. The staff is incredibly kind, the coffee AMAZING. (Got the French Toast and Turtle latte) Also some of the money goes to a great cause. Happily will visit again next time I am in Miami.

reviewSample.text: Five stars! The Tuna Toast was incredibly fresh and delicious. Crema Gourmet has such a great vibe and the service was excellent. Perfect spot for a quick bite or a chill hangout. Highly recommend!

Not a great experience overall. The place wasn’t even busy, yet the service felt completely disorganized despite having plenty of staff around. The food came out fresh 👍 but I waited forever for my iced coffee and eventually had to get up and ask for it again. I also asked for a printed receipt and never received it.

On top of that, their payment system automatically adds a tip and then still prompts you for an additional tip while staff stares. And honestly, the iced coffee was terrible and definitely not worth the wait.

I read the reviews during my meal and then regretted coming here… A bug in the food and all they do is apologize on the review? Wow.

Créma should have been the best place for coffee. It was not. Not for coffee nor for food.
The coffee was awful, the Greek salad was a shame to the Greek cuisine and the tuna sandwich was served in a baguette which has seen better days.(maybe it was tasty yesterday)
However the stuff tried its best to improve the feeling,
but if the foundation isn’t good, no service in the world will help.

It is a good place if you are just meeting someone for coffee or planning to work on your laptop. Plenty of seating space indoors and outdoors. There are power plugs inside but only in the corners. Chairs and couches are very comfortable. The staff is friendly. Love the atmosphere. Will definitely come back!

Among the best coffee cafes I’ve experienced, with a selection of roasted beans from across the globe. I like the specifications assigned to each bean selection, including acidity, aroma, flavor etc. The baked goods are also terrific.

reviewSample.text: We absolutely loved this place. The coffee, sandwiches, and pastries were all amazing, and everything tasted fresh and high quality. The atmosphere was beautiful, clean, and super cozy, perfect for studying, working, or just having a relaxing coffee date. Even though it was packed, the vibe still felt calm and welcoming. You can definitely tell why this spot stays busy. We’ll 100% be coming back. ☕️✨

Today was my first time at The Coffee Wynwood, i drove 25min to try this place since i heard good things about it and i m a Coffee lover, so i had a really high expectation!

I ordered an espresso, my friend a Macchiato and we shared a pastry, the pan swiss.

The pan swiss was good but it seemed like a bit undercooked, i really didn't love the texture of it.
The coffee beans thay use are for sure great quality but the espresso flavor was a bit too strong and and really dark on color, bit that s my personal impression, i m not saying it is not good!

The macchiato really disappointed me and my friend, didn't look good at all since the begging.
The milk was not creamy at all, there was too much air in it, and the texture was not the way it should be.

The place is minimalist and nice, i really liked it, the price are fair to be honest!
There is no service, since you order buly yourself and the table were "dirty" from the guests who left the table before we set.

I liked some how and i might give another chance to this place, but the first impression disappointed me.

I came over the weekend and thought the place was nicely decorated. It’s a great place to come, eat and study or work. Ordering food is also very easy on the tablet. I love how they offer all day brunch. I tried the omelette sandwich with a salad and it was delicious. The tasty omelette sandwich on brioche bread was big and filling. The salad was simple but delicious. The staff were also nice. I would definitely return to this location to try other items on the menu.

This coffee is straight fire!  When I went to pick it up, they let me watch them roast my beans fresh right there in front of me. You can definitely feel all the love and passion they put into their work. Highly recommend!

I called to ask a few questions about partnering and about private label . When the owner himself if rude and lies to you about private label  service that says alot about the company. I havent tasted coffee yet im sure its good but this guy looks down on small business. Milan thanks being a jerk. It opened my eyes on how you operate.

reviewSample.text:
Cafe Grumpy is exactly the kind of coffee shop you want as a go to, simple, confident, and consistently done right. They use organic valley milk, the baristas are genuinely kind, and the whole place has that calm energy that makes you want to stay a little longer.

The coffee is the real reason though. They have single origin options and a light roast that tastes clean and bright, with zero bitterness, just smooth, intentional flavor. Great location, super friendly service, and the kind of spot that becomes part of your routine fast.

Liz and Alex were so incredibly nice ans patient with us as we wanted our coffee “for here” post order.

More on social media: @ratedbymaryam

Such a cute coffee shop. I got a vanilla matcha and it was yummyyy, as well as their avocado toast. they have some really cute posters on the wall & a good bit of seats, to sit down and catch up with a friend. street parking is easy to find.

Quaint spot! Loved it. Alex made a perfect latte & iced matcha. The gallery wall is a nice way to casually shop local art. The music volume was ideal and the playlist tasteful - a rarity in Miami. I will be working here again.

reviewSample.text: It’s a decent spot to grab a quick takeaway coffee, especially if you’re already nearby, but did the coffee really stand out? Not really. It’s tucked inside an office building, so if you’re visiting Miami and want to explore cool or unique spots, this place definitely isn’t it. Plus, it didn’t seem like they roast their own beans, which kind of took away from the experience.

Coffee was Ok but very small size for the price. Almost 7usd for a cappuccino that is smaller than my hand - also theres a 1usd extra for non dairy milk. Id go if I had to but wouldnt make a point to come back

This location is inside an office building. The building had some type of event going on, so there were many young professionals running around mingling. Espresso is a specialty brew from Vice City. Creamy. A little bitterness and citrus hit your tongue right away. Great cup of espresso. They make their own pastries, so I ordered a carrot cake. It was just right for a morning cake. Not over the top indulgent as it should be for after dinner. Rather, pleasant, mild, sweet just enough with walnuts and shaved carrots inside. Although it was busy, the barista was very pleasant and answered my questions promptly.

reviewSample.text: Bro I love this place the tea’s are so good. The staff is amazing, especially Mike, Juandi and Matt and it’s such a chill area to hang out to just relax and detox from the day

Chris absolutely made the experience at Syndicate. His energy was on point, super attentive without being overbearing, and he really knew how to keep the vibes right the whole time. You can tell he genuinely cares about people having a great time.
The kavatendar was amazing! He helped me navigate the menu to find a great drink. The atmosphere was cozy and easy to talk in. Will definitely be back woth other friends!

reviewSample.text:
Quiet, stylish coffee spot in Coconut Grove with a very New York-style atmosphere. Perfect place to sit at the bar with your laptop, have a relaxed breakfast, or meet for a business coffee without feeling rushed.
Service is fast, the healthy breakfast options look amazing, and the coffee is honestly 10/10. If you prefer a creamier coffee, go for the latte; if you like a stronger coffee taste, the cappuccino is perfect. Most importantly, the coffee is served at the right temperature -- not overheated like in many places.
Beautiful interior design, cozy outdoor seating, and since it's not located directly on a busy road, it stays calm and quiet outside too. Definitely one of those spots that makes you want to stay longer.

This place has a lot of potential — cute setup, nice vibe, and a good amount of staff — but the service just doesn’t match the energy. Tables and counters tend to stay cluttered after people leave, which is a bit frustrating considering the limited seating. It gives off the impression that clearing up isn’t much of a priority.

Coffee-wise, it’s solid! I’d give it a 4/5 — enjoyable, but not the kind that makes you want to go out of your way for it. Personally, I think Navi Coffee, just nearby, does it a little better.

Overall, not a bad stop if you’re already in the area, but there’s definitely room for improvement.

Emissary Coffee is a wonderful little spot tucked away in Miami — perfect for anyone looking to slow down and enjoy a quality cup. The coffee is strong and well-crafted, exactly what a serious coffee lover wants. What really sets it apart is the warm, cozy atmosphere that makes you want to linger, and the dog-friendly vibe is a lovely touch. Whether you're coming solo with a book or bringing your pup along, it's an easy place to settle into and stay awhile. Highly recommend

reviewSample.text:
Café Du Bey completely surprised me. A lot of places with this kind of dreamy, tropical, fully outdoor setup don’t always get the coffee right. This one absolutely does.

The space feels like you’ve been dropped into Costa Rica. Lush greenery, layered natural textures, eclectic décor that somehow all flows, and an energy that feels vibrant but grounded. It’s all outdoor, airy, and alive. You instantly want to stay awhile.

They’re pulling a light roast, which you don’t see everywhere, and they execute it beautifully. Sherry walked us through the background of the coffee and the matcha with so much knowledge and pride. She made a perfect long black, clean and nuanced. The flat whites were balanced with silky texture, and we liked it so much we ordered a second cortado. That says everything.

We also tried the chai matcha and the ube matcha, both smooth, vibrant, and not overly sweet. The chicken empanadas were fresh, clean, and satisfying without feeling heavy.

Huge shoutout to Sherry for elevating the entire experience. Great coffee, thoughtful service, and a magical outdoor vibe. This place delivers!!

More on social media: @ratedbymaryam
Long brew with steamed milk, long brew black, cortado. Nice vibe. Friendly service. Great start to the day
Extraordinary coffee, genuine service, and tranquil environment. Probably the best coffee shop in Miami-Dade.

This place clearly put "try our macha" sign but actually do NOT have matcha. The worst was I waited for 10min before I was told that they didn't have matcha.  The only girl working there was too busy to even talk to me and told me to wait until she's ready for me. And off course when I told her I wanted matcha, she said, "We dont have matcha".... uuggghh!

Update: they purposely left the sign (a portable plastic tabletop banner that easily removed) "try our matcha" on the counter eventhough they "knew" they run out of matcha. I went back half an hour later the sign was still there. I then confronted the worker for the misleading sign. She just smiled and still kept the sign. reviewSample.text: Dropped by after a long day and the experience genuinely lifted my mood. The employee at the counter was patient while I decided what to order, and the mocha turned out excellent. Small details like clean tables and prompt service made a difference.

A nice cafe with some good vibes away from the hustle slightly down the road in Brickell. This is like the more local cafe spot that people come to.

The latte I got was good, its just your standard latte. No specific flavor or aroma to mention. There are also snacks as well, but overall a simple menu of items for easy selection.

The cafe itself is lovely. 2 Floors. There isnt tons of seating, but when there is, you can sit a long time to study, chat, or work. The wifi is great and pinged me up to 300 mbps at most on my phone. It was perfect to read a book. Service was great and restroom was spacious a clean. Easily a go to spot to focus.

I usually just go for an iced latte with regular milk, and that keeps me happy =) I love working from here—the second level has a wall-mounted table with high chairs, perfect for sitting or working while standing. The crew is great and super friendly!

reviewSample.text: When I tell you I’ve been looking for good Matcha in south Florida for sooooo long and I’m so happy I finally found it at Aura. Not just good, but GREAT. The different flavors are so tasty. The cold foam is delicious, it tastes like ice cream. Lately I’ve been just ordering the plain matcha on the rocks and it truly beats out any matcha I’ve ever had. The service is always great as the baristas are super friendly and helpful. And there’s a lot of cute photo ops at the location as well. I loved their matcha so much that I purchased the 100g bag so I could make it at home to hold me over until I can go back. They source their matcha from Japan and it is the real deal ceremonial grade. Soooo good!

Aura uses the highest quality ingredients, and it truly shows. This is hands down the best matcha I’ve had in a very long time! The flavor is smooth, rich, and vibrant without any bitterness. You can immediately tell it’s sourced with care from Japan. On top of that, their service is so warm and welcoming. Aura honestly puts every other matcha I’ve tried to shame. If you care about quality, this is the place to try!!!

Good matcha, I like the variety of flavours. I found it was overpriced for the quality and size but that’s Miami Beach for ya lol. The girls working were really friendly and helpful. The cafe is in the lobby of a hotel so there wasn’t much seating.

reviewSample.text:
Great coffee and even better service!

This morning, I stopped by Miami Joe Coffee Co. for a quick coffee and ended up leaving with a big smile thanks to the amazing service. From the moment I walked in, Pedro, the barista, made my day—he greeted me warmly with a genuine smile and respectful eye contact. His friendly and professional attitude really stood out and created a welcoming start to the morning.

I ordered a coffee and a chocolate croissant, and both were excellent. The coffee was rich and smooth, and the croissant was fresh, flaky, and just the right amount of chocolatey. The space itself is cozy, clean, and a great spot to relax or grab a quick bite.

It’s those small, genuine moments of kindness like Pedro’s that turn a regular coffee run into something memorable. I’ll definitely be coming back!

Delicious Tunacado (order it with Jalapeños for a nice kick) and even tastier Acai bowls!
Get the app, enjoy within 2 min walk of the beach

I love this café, service is really good, food is delicious and ambiance is excellent to spend a time while you’re drinking a coffee. As an açaí lover, definitely this is my açaí bowl’s spot. Granola is super good!
